title: Kimchi Pancakes Kimchijeon

seo.title: Crispy Kimchi Pancakes (Kimchijeon) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make crispy, savory Kimchi Pancakes (Kimchijeon) at home with this easy Korean recipe. Perfect as an appetizer or snack!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Kimchijeon
seo.imageAlt: A stack of golden-brown crispy Kimchi Pancakes (Kimchijeon) garnished with green onions and dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy these savory and spicy Kimchi Pancakes, a popular Korean appetizer known as Kimchijeon, perfect for any meal!

description: Kimchi Pancakes, or Kimchijeon, are a beloved Korean savory pancake featuring fermented kimchi, creating a delightful balance of spicy, sour, and umami flavors. These crispy-edged, tender-centered pancakes are incredibly satisfying as a snack, appetizer, or a light meal. Easy to customize and quick to make, they are a fantastic way to enjoy the vibrant taste of Korean cuisine right in your own kitchen.

ingredients.notes: or as needed for batter consistency
Korean chili flakes, optional, for dipping sauce

instructions.title: Prepare Kimchi and Vegetables
instructions.text: Dice the aged kimchi into small, bite-sized pieces. Thinly slice the yellow onion and chop the green onions. Set aside. Ensure your kimchi is well-fermented for optimal flavor.

instructions.title: Make the Pancake Batter
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, salt, and sugar. Gradually whisk in the kimchi juice and water until a smooth batter forms. It should be thick enough to coat a spoon but still pourable. Add the egg and whisk until fully incorporated.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients for Pancakes
instructions.text: Add the diced kimchi, sliced yellow onion, and chopped green onions into the batter. Mix gently until all ingredients are evenly distributed. Do not overmix, as this can make the pancakes tough.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and toasted sesame seeds. If desired, add a pinch of gochugaru for a spicy kick. Set aside.

instructions.title: Heat Pan and Fry First Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 1-2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Once the oil is shimmering, pour about 1/2 to 3/4 cup of the batter into the hot pan, spreading it evenly with the back of a spoon to form a thin, round pancake.

instructions.title: Cook Until Golden and Crispy
instructions.text: Cook the pancake for 3-5 minutes per side, or until it is golden brown and crispy on the edges. Press down lightly with a spatula occasionally to ensure even cooking and crispiness. Flip carefully and cook the other side until similarly golden and cooked through.

instructions.title: Repeat and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the cooked pancake from the pan and place it on a wire rack or paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil. Add more oil to the pan as needed and repeat the frying process with the remaining batter. Slice the pancakes into wedges and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: For extra crispiness, use a combination of flour and a small amount of rice flour or cornstarch in your batter. Also, ensure your pan is hot enough before pouring the batter.
Always use well-fermented, sour kimchi for the best flavor in Kimchijeon. If your kimchi isn't sour enough, add a small splash of vinegar to the batter.
Kimchi Pancakes are best enjoyed immediately while hot and crispy. If you have leftovers, reheat them in a dry pan or an air fryer to regain some crispness.
article: Kimchi Pancakes Kimchijeon is a beloved Korean Appetizers dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
